hints.Israel: 'Israel' is a proper noun, the name Israel
hints.suum: 'Suum' is a possessive adjective meaning 'his/its own'

hints.to receive: That would be 'accipere'; 'suscipere' means 'to take up/support'

steps.reveal: suscepit = he has taken up / he upheld

hints.present indicative: Present would be 'suscipit'; 'suscepit' with changed vowel is perfect

hints.capio, capere, cepi, captum: These are the principal parts of 'capio' (to take)
hints.accipio, accipere, accepi, acceptum: These are the principal parts of 'accipio' (to receive)
hints.recipio, recipere, recepi, receptum: These are the principal parts of 'recipio' (to recover)

hints.nominative: Israel is the object being upheld, not the subject; the subject is God (implied)
hints.genitive: 'Israel' is indeclinable; context shows it as the direct object
hints.ablative: 'Israel' is indeclinable; context shows it as the direct object

steps.reveal: puerum = servant/child (acc., in apposition to Israel)

hints.nominative: Nominative would be 'puer'; 'puerum' with -um is accusative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns end in -a; 'puer' has genitive 'pueri'
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension has -is in the genitive; 'puer' has genitive 'pueri'

translation: He has helped Israel his servant
